title: Cobalt, copper and controls: DRC Regional Spotlight
gist: Paywalled teaser: the DRC dominates mined cobalt (three quarters of 2025 supply) and is the second-largest copper supplier (15% in 2025), built on 15 years of Chinese investment. It also hosts the Manono hard-rock lithium deposit, where Zijin's Manono North (120ktpa) is now operating and an AVZ-owned, CATL-backed project (90ktpa) targets 2032.

insights.claim: The Democratic Republic of Congo supplied three quarters of the world's mined cobalt in 2025, making it the leading global cobalt supplier.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Concentration this extreme means any DRC disruption or export control directly sets cobalt price and battery-supply risk; watch DRC policy as a single point of failure.

insights.claim: The Democratic Republic of Congo was the world's second-largest supplier of mined copper in 2025, providing 15% of global supply.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: DRC copper is now a material share of the electrification supply chain, so its output and control regime affect copper balances beyond cobalt alone.

insights.claim: The DRC's rise as a major cobalt and copper supplier followed significant Chinese investment over the last 15 years.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Chinese ownership over DRC output ties Western battery-metal security to Chinese supply-chain control; a signal to track for reshoring and export-control risk.

insights.claim: Zijin's Manono North lithium project in the DRC's Tanganyika Province is currently operating and has an eventual planned capacity of 120ktpa.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: New hard-rock lithium coming online adds African supply and further embeds Chinese ownership (Zijin) in the lithium chain; track ramp toward 120ktpa.
insights.quote: Manono North, owned by Zijin, is currently operating and has an eventual planned capacity of 120ktpa.

insights.claim: A separate AVZ-owned, CATL-backed Manono lithium project is due to come online in 2032 with a planned capacity of 90ktpa.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: A 2032 timeline signals this second Manono tranche is a long-dated option, not near-term supply; CATL backing flags where future offtake is being locked up.
insights.quote: A separate, AVZ-owned and CATL-backed Manono project is due to come online in 2032, with a planned capacity of 90ktpa.
